top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic TjejVättern's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
TjejVättern's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
TjejVättern's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
At TjejVättern, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of TjejVättern. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At TjejVättern, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what TjejVättern's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for TjejVättern’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at TjejVättern requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Motala, Sweden.
